description: A credential pasted directly into source or config is compromised the moment it is committed and exposes every system it unlocks.
body: Failure mode: an API key, password, or token is written into source or a tracked config to make a feature work, then committed. Detection signals: a scanner flags a high-entropy or pattern-matching string; a key value appears in a diff; the same value is reused across files. Do not leave a hardcoded secret in place or merely delete it without rotating. Safer fallback: rotate the exposed credential immediately, move the secret to a manager or environment variable, fetch it at runtime, scrub history after rotation, and escalate the exposure to its owner.
